data.acceptance:
AC1. knots/docs/leases.md, knots/README.md, foolery/docs/knots-agent-identity-contract.md, and foolery/docs/adr-knots-compatibility.md describe the lease as the declared source of agent identity for a claim. No "fallback" framing remains anywhere in those docs.
AC2. knots/TAXONOMY.md reflects the same framing. Grep over the knots codebase for the substrings "fallback" and "or_else" in the context of agent identity returns no results that describe lease as a fallback; any remaining uses are unrelated.
AC3. For every kno subcommand except `kno lease create`, the --agent-name / --agent-model / --agent-version flags (plus --note-agentname / --note-model / --note-version on update and --handoff-agentname / --handoff-model / --handoff-version on update) are accepted syntactically but ignored at runtime. Passing any of them emits a three-line stderr warning: (a) the flag is deprecated and will be rejected in a future release, (b) its value is ignored because identity comes from the bound lease, (c) if no lease is bound, instruction to create one and pass --lease on claim.
AC4. Knots stamps agent_name, agent_model, and agent_version from the bound lease's agent_info onto: step_history entries created by `kno next`, `kno rollback`, `kno claim`, and `kno poll --claim`; note entries created by `kno update --add-note`; handoff capsule entries created by `kno update --add-handoff-capsule`; gate decision metadata created by `kno gate evaluate`; step annotations created by `kno step annotate`. If no lease is bound, those fields remain unset and the deprecation warning path applies.
AC5. CLI help text for every deprecated flag reads "[DEPRECATED — IGNORED] ..." so `kno <cmd> --help` surfaces the deprecation at first touch.
AC6. Any managed-skills prompt text or action_prompt template that instructs agents to invoke --agent-name / --agent-model / --agent-version on commands other than `kno lease create` is removed. Agents following kno's own prompts do not end up violating the contract.
AC7. In the Foolery client, `src/lib/knots-operations.ts` passes agent metadata flags only inside `createLease`. `claimKnot`, `pollKnot`, `appendNoteArgs`, and `appendHandoffArgs` pass none. The hardcoded "foolery" string that previously defaulted --note-agentname / --handoff-agentname is removed. Callers in `src/lib/execution-backend-helpers.ts` and `src/lib/backends/knots-backend-prompts.ts` stop threading agent fields into claim/poll/update.
AC8. `cargo fmt && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings && cargo test` passes in ~/knots. `bun run lint && bunx tsc --noEmit && bun run test && bun run build` passes in ~/foolery.
